name: Dusty Fletcher
abstract: Clinton "Dusty" Fletcher (July 8, 1900 – March 15, 1954) was an African-American vaudeville performer and comedian, who was best known for the comedy routine which became a hit record in 1947, "Open the Door, Richard".

concerts.label: Jazz Concert featuring Frankie Laine, Ella Fitzgerald, Louis Jordan, and Woody Herman
Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert featuring Frankie Laine, Ella Fitzgerald, Louis Jordan, and Woody Herman

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I used to listen to those records and be fascinated by the music... which had, you know, Duke Ellington, Billie Holliday, Count Basie, Slim Gaillard, Dusty Fletcher.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: That was recorded ...we were in Cafe Society at the time I did all those things. Yeah. ... 'Open the Door Richard' was a comedian, Dusty Fletcher, you see. ... and that's how I did that with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: where he used to play, aong with Fletcher... they had entire revue reviews... like (unintelligible) Fletcher 'Open the Door, Richard.'

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: And the old timers like Pigmeat, Pigmeat Markham, Dusty Fletcher. Gee whiz, I can't think of the rest of them.

about_quotes.speaker: Erskine Hawkins
about_quotes.evidence: We was at, Dusty Fletcher was at the Strand with me, one of my engagements at the Strand.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: one of the stars was a comedian known as Dusty Fletcher whom I had seen many times.
